proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
Prior cohort_promotion_judge approval on 2026-04-29 for the same candidate is consistent with this decision; no disagreement.
Bear regime is currently absent (0 of last 7 days) but was present 4 of prior 7 days — regime is cyclical, so promotion will become live on next Bear rotation.
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
When Bear regime returns, UP_TRI exposure (currently 16 of 25 open positions) combined with this boost could amplify concentration; portfolio_exposure caps should remain enforced at execution time.
R-multiple 0.61 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7; if exit policy changes, cohort R-multiple may shift and tier should be re-evaluated.
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +30.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +30.9pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. The cohort is regime-gated to Bear, so it will not activate against the current Choppy-concentrated portfolio — concentration risk is deferred until regime rotates. No prior trader rejection on record for this cohort.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +24.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)

Prior approval for same cohort (Metal × UP_TRI) on 2026-04-29 with n=15, 100% WR; current candidate adds 2 trades and remains in Tier M with stable Wilson lower bound, indicating cohort signal is persisting rather than mean-reverting
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime and UP_TRI-heavy (16/25); any new Metal × UP_TRI signal that boosts will compound this concentration — trader should weigh sector exposure before acting on TAKE_SMALL boost
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review pending); if exit policy changes, R-multiple may shift and tier could be revisited
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +24.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +24.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than broadens the existing UP_TRI overweight, and a prior similar candidate for this exact cohort was approved on 2026-04-29 with consistent reasoning.

proposals.title: Regime shift: Bear → Choppy

claim.expected_effect: A fresh weekly intelligence update (current snapshot is 5 days old) confirming slope remains non-negative and avg_ret20 holds would solidify the Choppy classification vs a transient bounce within a Bear regime.

evidence: Recent 7d distribution: Choppy 7/7 vs prior 7d: Bear 4/7, Choppy 3/7 (majority flipped from Bear to Choppy)
Weekly intelligence slope positive (0.003614) with avg_ret20=7.38 and price above EMA50, consistent with exit from Bear
regime_stability classified as 'stable' on the new Choppy regime, indicating the shift has consolidated
risks: Detection may be one-week wobble misclassified as transition
Trader manual confirmation required; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational alert; no system mutation. Auto-suppresses on next run if regime stabilizes.

transition_evidence: Recent 7d distribution: Choppy 7/7 vs prior 7d: Bear 4/7, Choppy 3/7 (majority flipped from Bear to Choppy)
Weekly intelligence slope positive (0.003614) with avg_ret20=7.38 and price above EMA50, consistent with exit from Bear
regime_stability classified as 'stable' on the new Choppy regime, indicating the shift has consolidated
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Recent 7-day window is unanimously Choppy (7/7) while the prior 7-day window was majority Bear (4/7) with only 3 Choppy days, confirming a Bear-to-Choppy transition. Days_in_current_regime is 7, still within the 14-day alert window, and the shift is supported by weekly intelligence showing positive slope (0.003614), avg_ret20 of 7.38, and price above EMA50.
